wine_museums.name: Cite des Climats et vins de Bourgogne Beaune
wine_museums.address: 21 Avenue Charles de Gaulle, 21200 Beaune, France
wine_museums.description: The Beaune flagship of the three-site Cite des Climats network, opened 2023, with an immersive trail telling Burgundy's wine identity from Chablis to the Maconnais, a tasting bar and workshop rooms.
wine_museums.admission: Discovery trail ticket; combined visit-and-tasting packages available

wine_museums.name: Hotel-Dieu des Hospices de Beaune
wine_museums.description: Former charitable almshouse founded 1443 by Nicolas Rolin, with its iconic glazed-tile roof. The museum traces the Hospices' history and its still-working wine estate famous for the November charity auction.
wine_museums.admission: Museum entry from around EUR 12; last admission one hour before closing

wine_museums.name: Cite des Climats et vins de Bourgogne Chablis
wine_museums.address: 1 bis Rue de Chichee, 89800 Chablis, France
wine_museums.description: The Chablis site of the Cite des Climats network in the village centre, dedicated to the heritage and culture of Chablis Chardonnay, with exhibits, a tasting bar and guided workshops near the vineyards.
wine_museums.admission: Discovery trail ticket; combined visit-and-tasting packages available

wine_museums.name: Cite des Climats et vins de Bourgogne Macon
wine_museums.address: 520 Avenue Marechal de Lattre de Tassigny, 71000 Macon, France
wine_museums.description: The Macon site of the Cite des Climats network inside the renovated BIVB premises, presenting the history of southern Burgundy along the Saone with a visitor trail, tasting bar and workshops.
wine_museums.admission: Discovery trail ticket; combined visit-and-tasting packages available

verified.address_quoted: 520 av. Marechal de Lattre de Tassigny 71000, Macon

wine_museums.name: Le Cassissium
wine_museums.address: 8 Passage des Freres Montgolfier, 21700 Nuits-Saint-Georges, France
wine_museums.description: Visitor centre and museum at the Vedrenne liqueur house in Nuits-Saint-Georges, telling the story of blackcurrant and Burgundy's creme de cassis across the centuries, ending in a guided tasting.
wine_museums.admission: Guided visit with tasting; reservations mandatory
